questions.question: Explain the differences between while, do-while, and for loops in C with suitable examples.
keyPoints: while loop checks condition before execution - may execute zero times
do-while checks condition after execution - always executes at least once
for loop is ideal when number of iterations is known in advance
for loop syntax includes initialization, condition, and increment in one statement
while and do-while are better when iterations depend on runtime conditions

questions.question: What is the purpose of break and continue statements in loops? Explain with examples.
keyPoints: break terminates the loop immediately and transfers control outside the loop
continue skips the current iteration and moves to the next iteration
break is used to exit loop prematurely based on certain conditions
continue is used to skip processing certain values
Both help in controlling loop flow efficiently

questions.question: Write a C program to print all even numbers from 1 to 50 using a for loop.
keyPoints: Use for loop with initialization i=2
Condition should be i<=50
Increment should be i+=2 to get only even numbers
Alternatively use i++ and check i%2==0 inside loop
Print each even number

questions.question: What is an infinite loop? How can it be created and how can it be terminated?
keyPoints: Infinite loop runs forever without terminating
Can be created using for(;;), while(1), or do-while(1)
Also occurs when loop condition is always true
Can be terminated using break statement
Or by external intervention like Ctrl+C

questions.question: Explain nested loops with an example to print a multiplication table.
keyPoints: Nested loop is a loop inside another loop
Outer loop controls rows, inner loop controls columns
For multiplication table 1-10, use nested for loops
Inner loop multiplies outer loop variable with inner loop variable
Example: for i=1 to 10, for j=1 to 10, print i*j

questions.question: Write a C program to calculate the sum of digits of a given number using a while loop.
keyPoints: Extract last digit using num%10
Add digit to sum
Remove last digit using num/10
Repeat until number becomes zero
Display the sum

questions.question: Differentiate between pre-increment (++i) and post-increment (i++) in the context of for loops.
keyPoints: ++i increments before using the value
i++ increments after using the value
In for loop increment expression, both produce same effect
Difference matters when used in other expressions
++i is slightly more efficient as it doesn't store old value

questions.question: Write a complete C program to check whether a given number is prime or not using loops. Explain the logic.
keyPoints: Prime number is divisible only by 1 and itself
Start checking divisibility from 2 to sqrt(n)
Use a flag variable to track if divisor found
If any divisor found, number is not prime
Optimize by checking only odd numbers after 2
Use for loop or while loop for iteration

questions.question: Write a C program to generate the Fibonacci series up to n terms. Explain the algorithm and its time complexity.
keyPoints: Fibonacci: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8... each term is sum of two previous terms
Initialize first two terms as 0 and 1
Use loop to generate subsequent terms
Print each term and update variables for next iteration
Time complexity is O(n) - linear
Space complexity is O(1) with iterative approach

questions.question:
Write a C program to display the following pattern using nested loops:
* * * * *
* * * * *
* * * * *
Explain the logic behind nested loops for pattern printing.
keyPoints: Use two nested loops - outer for rows, inner for columns
Outer loop runs 3 times for 3 rows
Inner loop runs 5 times for 5 stars per row
Print star followed by space in inner loop
Print newline after completing each row
Modify loops for different patterns

questions.question: Write a C program to find the factorial of a number using different loop types. Compare their implementations.
keyPoints: Factorial n! = n × (n-1) × (n-1) × ... × 1
Using for loop: initialize product=1, multiply from 1 to n
Using while loop: similar logic with while condition
Using do-while: guarantee at least one multiplication
Handle edge case of 0! = 1
All three have O(n) time complexity
